attacking_style: Launches long-range solo attacks (20+ km) on mountain stages, typically from breakaways or on final climbs. Shows willingness to go early and alone rather than wait for final kilometers.
team_tactics: Performs best when freed from domestique duties and given protected status. Previously constrained by team role at INEOS, requiring liberty to attack to maximize effectiveness.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with opportunities for long-distance efforts. Thrives when able to establish gaps on extended climbs rather than punchy finale sprints.
vulnerability: Finale positioning and bonus seconds management - lost Grand Tour lead purely on bonus second deficit despite matching time. Weak in technical finales requiring precise placement.
key_patterns: Executes long-distance solo attacks (20+ km) when given freedom, evidenced across both Grand Tours
Requires protected team leader status to perform optimally, not effective as domestique
Struggles with tactical positioning in sprint finales, losing crucial bonus seconds
Attacks from large breakaways on final climbs rather than GC group (32-rider break stage win)
form_trajectory: positive: evolved from constrained domestique role to stage winner when given leadership freedom in 2022 Vuelta
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
